stories.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine and fresh Russia sanctions – as it happened
stories.summary: Move comes after Hungary and Slovakia dropped opposition following reopening of the Druzhba oil pipeline Meanwhile, Ukraine’s president Volodymyr Zelenskiy said that Kyiv will seek to receive the first tranche of the €90bn European Union loan by the end of May, or early June. “This is strengthening of our army,” he told reporters in a WhatsApp chat, reported by Reuters. Continue reading...

stories.title: The Guardian view on Anthropic’s Claude Mythos: when AI finds every flaw, who controls the internet? | Editorial
stories.summary: Tech can scale cyber-attacks and defences alike, raising questions about private power, public risk and the future of a shared internet Anthropic announced its latest AI model, Claude Mythos , this month but said it would not be released publicly, because it turns computers into crime scenes. The company claimed that it could find previously unknown “zero-day” flaws, exploit them and, in principle, link these weaknesses in order to take over major operating systems and web browsers . Mythos did

stories.title: Orbán may be out, but Patriots aren’t changing course
stories.summary: Europe’s far-right Patriots for Europe gathered in Portugal this week to project strength after Viktor Orbán’s election defeat in Hungary and to double down on their call for tougher migration policies. Orbán had been widely seen as the group’s founding force , but following his ouster from government, attention is shifting to a new duo: France’s Jordan Bardella and Portugal’s André Ventura. Both used this week’s event to declare their political “friendship” and shared mission as the

stories.title: EU forges ahead with membership for Ukraine and Moldova after Orbán’s exit
stories.summary: AYIA NAPA, Cyprus — Ukraine and Moldova will be able to move forward with the process of joining the EU following Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s election defeat, diplomats and officials said, ending a four-year stalemate over their applications. The two countries have seen their path to membership delayed after being granted candidate status in 2022, with Orbán vetoing the formal initiation of negotiating “clusters” — the groups of reforms and undertakings candidate countries have to ac

stories.title: EU approves €90B loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― The EU agreed to release the €90 billion loan to Ukraine on Thursday following months of tense negotiations. “The European support loan for Ukraine has been unblocked ― €90 billion over two years,” Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy wrote on X . Hungary and Slovakia ― who had previously blocked the financial package ― failed to object before a 3 p.m. deadline, clearing the final hurdle to approve the loan. The two countries lifted their vetoes after oil flows through

stories.title: EU sanctions more Russian banks to squeeze Putin’s war economy
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU governments have banned another 20 Russian lenders from handling payments in euros and doing business with companies across the bloc under the latest package of sanctions in response to President Vladimir Putin’s war on Ukraine. António Costa, president of the European Council, on Thursday announced the sanctions package, the 20th of its kind since Moscow’s full-scale invasion in February 2022, after Hungary and Slovakia withdrew their veto against the punitive measures. “Eur

stories.title: EU formally approves €90bn Ukraine loan and 20th sanctions package against Russia
stories.summary: Ursula von der Leyen hails ‘good news’ after Hungary’s lifting of vetoes allows leaders to sign off on agreements EU leaders have welcomed the end of diplomatic deadlock over a long-awaited €90bn (£78bn) loan for Ukraine, after the bloc completed the agreement along with a 20th sanctions package against Russia. After weeks of delay, the EU signed off on the loan on Thursday, in time for a summit in Cyprus that began in the evening and will include talks over a dinner with the Ukrainian leader, V

stories.title: Newsletter: EU leaders weigh a NATO-style common defence clause
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan and new Russia sanctions.

stories.title: Jogi atombombát jelentene az országnak, ha május 13-án véget érne a veszélyhelyzet
stories.summary: Több mint 150 rendelet vesztené hatályát, megszűnne a benzin- és élelmiszerárstop, de változna a javítóintézetek vagy a gödi akkumulátorgyár helyzete is. Ez azt is mutatja, miért nem jó, ha nem arra használják a veszélyhelyzetet, amire való. Különleges jogrendben élünk 2020 óta. Magyar azt kérte a távozó Orbán-kormánytól, hogy ezt május 31-ig hosszabbítsák meg.

stories.title: A magyar közélet még azt a közösséget is képes volt felbolygatni, aminek a célja az olvasás népszerűsítése
stories.summary: A TikTok köré szerveződő magyar közösség egyre nagyobb és népszerűbb, a magyar BookTok most megtartja az első fesztiválját, de nem mindenki örömére.

stories.title: His dance brought him global fame, but Zsolt Hegedűs wants to be known for reforming the ailing Hungarian healthcare system
stories.summary: The orthopedic specialist known for his Travolta-like moves plans to introduce the best elements of the British healthcare system as Minister of Health in the Tisza government.

stories.summary: Az egyhangúlag jóváhagyott írásbeli eljárásban senki sem tiltakozott 24 órán belül, így elfogadták a jogszabályt, amelyet a magyar kormány az ukrán kőolajtranzit újraindulásáig blokkolt.

stories.title: Romániában életbe lép Európa legátfogóbb nőgyilkosság-törvénye
stories.summary: A jogszabály a tervek szerint a büntetés mellett a megelőzésre is hangsúlyt fektet majd.

stories.summary: A kormányzásról nem mondana le a párt, csak a miniszterelnököt távolítanák el.

stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök azt is közölte, hogy meg fogják nyitni az ügynökaktákat.

stories.summary: A magyar és a szlovák kormány az ukrán kőolajtranzit újraindulása miatt blokkolt, így csak két hónappal a tervezett határidő után tudták elfogadni az új büntetőintézkedéseket.

stories.title: Segít az erős forint, egyelőre olcsóbbak a nyári repülőjegyek, mint tavaly
stories.summary: A drága kerozin miatt egyre több légitársaság ritkítja járathálózatát, ugyanakkor a Magyarországról induló rövid távú nyári járatok többsége idén még mindig olcsóbb, mint tavaly.

stories.summary: A Magyar Nemzeti Bank közölte, hogy vizsgálatot indít a kérdéses időszakra vonatkozóan.

stories.summary: A kitermelési mennyiségek miatt indul vizsgálat a Hahót Tőzeg Kft.-vel szemben.

stories.summary: A leköszönő külgazdasági és külügyminisztert élőben, több mint három órán keresztül kérdezte Fábián Tamás.

stories.title: It took a massive propaganda campaign to turn Hungarians into potato lovers
stories.summary: Potatoes are such an important part of Hungarian cuisine that it is hard to imagine that as recently as two hundred years ago, people did not want to eat them.

stories.title: Egy évtizede gyűrik, a más uniós országokban dolgozó magyarok jogairól is szól egy új alku
stories.summary: Két bedőlt egyezség után ismét megállapodtak a szociális jogokról szóló javaslatról. Az általában a keleti munkaerőnél felmerülő „szociális dömping” ellen is fellépnének.

stories.summary: Melegh Noémi Napsugár több díj mellett az André Kertész Nagydíjat is elhozta, Bődey Jánost is kétszer díjazták.

stories.title: Ezek voltak 2025 legjobb magyar sajtófotói
stories.summary: Halmozottan sérült, autizmussal élő kisgyerekről készült sorozat, és az éneklő Magyar Péterről készült fotó is nyert az idei, sorban a 44. Sajtófotó Pályázaton, ahol a Telex fotósait is bőségesen díjazták.

stories.title: A magyarok 1945 óta törvénytelenül használják a földeket, mondta a szlovák kormány alelnöke
stories.summary: Robert Kaliňák a jövőbeni magyar-szlovák viszony egyik legkényesebb pontjáról, a Beneš-dekrétumokról beszélt.

stories.summary: A Magyar Helsinki Bizottság szerint Szabó Bence jogviszonyát szabályellenesen, anyagi és erkölcsi sérelmet okozva szüntette meg a rendőrség.

stories.title: Újabb minisztereket jelent be pénteken Magyar Péter
stories.summary: Köztük lesz a leendő oktatási miniszter is. Eddig kilenc minisztert jelentett be a hamarosan felálló Tisza-kormányból.

stories.title: Önök milliárdosok? – a Szijjártó-interjú legerősebb pillanatai
stories.summary: Az oroszokkal való „pragmatikus viszony”, KGBéla elengedése, a felesége több százmilliós nyereségű cége, Mészáros Lőrinc, jachtozás Szíjj Lászlóval és a személyes jövője a Fideszben. A Szijjártó Péterrel készült több mint háromórás interjúnk legfontosabb pillanatai.

stories.title: Ukrajna a fronton megállította az orosz gőzhengert, de a napi működéshez égető szüksége van az uniós hitelre
stories.summary: A Barátság olajvezeték megnyitásával kötötte össze a leköszönő Orbán-kormány a 90 milliárd eurós uniós hitelt, amit keddtől nem blokkol tovább. Az utolsó előtti pillanatban érkezhet meg a pénz, hogy ne kelljen áthidaló kölcsönt felvenniük az ukránoknak. A Tisza-kormány új lapot nyithat a magyar–ukrán viszonyban, de maradnak régi kérdések. Rácz Andrással elemeztük az elmúlt időszak történéseit.

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
stories.summary: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

stories.title: Nine lessons for the US from Viktor Orbán’s defeat | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: As US elections approach, the Hungarian prime minister’s loss is a reminder that history does not march relentlessly toward autocracy Viktor Orbán’s electoral loss was a slap in the face for Donald Trump and JD Vance, who had enthusiastically endorsed Europe’s most visible autocrat but proved unable to salvage his candidacy. But Hungarian voters’ 12 April rejection of Orbán also holds important lessons for Americans who hope to resist Trump’s own autocratic tendencies. As the November midterm el

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
stories.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

stories.title: Thousands call on UK ministers to cut ties with US tech giant Palantir
stories.summary: More than 200,000 have signed petitions urging the government to break contracts amid concerns about the company’s ‘supervillain’ manifesto More than 200,000 people have called on ministers to break contracts with Palantir in an apparent groundswell of public concern about the US tech company’s role in the NHS, police, military and councils. Two petitions have attracted 229,000 signatures, one calling for the government to end all public contracts with the company, the software of which is used

stories.title: Extended naval blockade is admission US military escalation poses even greater risk
stories.summary: Battle of the blockades may still have more time to run as the US and Iran try to assert control over the strait of Hormuz Donald Trump’s decision to extend the naval blockade of Iran indefinitely may do nothing to reduce world oil prices – but it could amount to a recognition that further US military escalation in breach of the nominal ceasefire comes with greater risk against a regime disinclined to surrender. In theory, Trump’s military options are increasing. A third US carrier strike group,

stories.title: Climat : sur la scène internationale, Monique Barbut assume de jouer petit bras
stories.summary: PARIS — Que se passe-t-il pour que la France n’envoie pas de ministre à la conférence de Santa Marta  sur la sortie des énergies fossiles qui se tient la semaine prochaine ? Et, alors que l’exécutif tricolore préside le G7 cette année, qu’il décide d’escamoter du menu des négociations des ministres de l’Environnement qui s’ouvre ce jeudi 23 avril l’énergie et la lutte contre le changement climatique ? Nommée au gouvernement en partie pour

stories.title: Italy’s migrant detention hubs in Albania not against EU law, says top EU court adviser
stories.summary: Italy’s migrant detention hubs in Albania are in line with EU law as long as migrants’ rights are fully protected, according to an adviser to the bloc’s top court. The legal opinion, which is not binding, is a win for the government of Giorgia Meloni, whose plans to process and deport asylum-seekers via offshore hubs have been embroiled in legal challenges. Italy’s center in Gjadër reportedly held 90 people in February, the highest number since Rome opened the hub, but fa

stories.title: Intimate talks: Brussels ponders the naked body in AI bill
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Diplomats don’t normally sit around discussing how much nudity is too much, but that’s what’s happening in Brussels as the European Union tries to clamp down on AI-powered nudification technology. Diplomats, bureaucrats and officials in past days have been mulling how to define “intimate parts” in a bill reforming the bloc’s AI Act, according to two people involved in the talks, who were granted anonymity to discuss confidential negotiations. The Eu

stories.title: UK allies dismayed by Mandelson vetting meltdown
stories.summary: LONDON — Peter Mandelson’s appointment as U.S. ambassador despite security concerns — and the subsequent dissection of that process in public — have caused consternation among Britain’s intelligence-sharing partners, according to two people briefed on the matter. stories.summary: Lebanese PM calls attack that killed Amal Khalil a ‘war crime’, with rescuers attempting to free her also targeted Middle East crisis – live updates Israel’s killing of a prominent Lebanese journalist in a double-tap strike has been greeted with international outrage as Lebanon’s prime minister described the attack as a “war crime”. stories.summary: Spain’s PM, Pedro Sánchez, hosted the inaugural meeting of the Global Progressive Mobilisation. Keir Starmer and other social democrats were notable by their absence Can progressives push back the rising tide of authoritarianism? Thousands of people gathered in Barcelona this weekend in search of an answer. stories.title: Bibby Stockholm asylum barge contractor admits overcharging UK government £118m

Corporate Travel Management (CTM) said its auditor had found evidence of “erroneous billing” of its UK clients, increasing its estimate of how much it owes the government by £40m. stories.summary: Forecasting service raises alarm over data from Paris airport used to settle Polymarket wagers on temperature French police are investigating alleged tampering with national weather forecasting service equipment after a series of unusual temperature readings coincided with suspicious winning bets made on Polymarket. stories.title: Landmark report details lasting impact of slavery in key Georgia county
stories.summary: Taskforce report examines how effects of slavery and Jim Crow in Fulton county continue to harm Black residents A Georgia taskforce has released a landmark report that details the lasting impact of slavery and its afterlives in Fulton county. The report , spanning more than 600 pages, is based on original research by the Fulton county reparations taskforce and a review of primary source documents. stories.title: Reza Pahlavi calls for regime change in Iran on Berlin visit
stories.summary: The former Shah's son is in Germany to drum up support for his global campaign to overthrow Iran's leadership. During a speech in the capital, he called the German government's refusal to meet with him "a disgrace."

stories.title: Pope Leo stressed importance of dialogue on Africa trip
stories.summary: During a visit to four African countries, Pope Leo XIV called for peace, unity and dialogue. He criticized war, corruption and inequality, urging leaders to put people first.

stories.title: ‘A history of the Earth’: Twelve Apostles revealed to be as old as 14m years

stories.summary: Duke of Sussex was speaking at the Kyiv Security Forum during a surprise visit to show support for Ukraine after four years of war with Russia. The prince added that it was also 'a moment for America to show that it can honour its international treaty obligations'. His last visit to the country was in September 2025, when he discussed support for war veterans Continue reading...

stories.title: Head-on train collision near Copenhagen leaves five critically injured
stories.summary: At least 18 people hurt after crash involving two local services north of Denmark’s capital Two trains have collided head-on in Denmark, injuring at least 18 people, five of whom are in a critical condition. The crash happened on Thursday morning at a level crossing at Isterødvejen, near Hillerød, a town about 19 miles (30km) north-west of Copenhagen. Emergency services received a report of the collision just before 6.30am. Continue reading...

stories.title: Is the tide turning for Ukraine in war with Russia? – The Latest
stories.summary: With the EU approving a €90bn loan for Ukraine, a surprise visit from Prince Harry, and data suggesting Russian troops made almost no territorial gains in March – are there reasons for optimism in Kyiv? Lucy Hough speaks to senior international correspondent Luke Harding – watch on YouTube Continue reading...

stories.title: Private health records of half a million Britons offered for sale on Chinese website
stories.summary: Technology minister tells Commons ‘de-identified’ information from UK Biobank advertised for sale on Alibaba The confidential health records of half a million British volunteers have been offered for sale on Chinese website Alibaba, the UK government has confirmed. The “de-identified” data, belonging to participants in the UK Biobank project, was found for sale on three separate listings last week. Ian Murray, the technology minister, told the Commons on Thursday that, after working with the Chi

stories.title: Dozens of Mexican mafia members arrested in California crackdown
stories.summary: Prosecutors say 43 people indicted on charges including murder, kidnapping, extortion and drug trafficking More than two dozen members and associates of the Mexican mafia were arrested during an early morning crackdown in southern California, federal authorities said on Thursday. The FBI and other federal and local agencies executed search and arrest warrants at locations mostly in Orange county, south of Los Angeles, according to the US attorney’s office. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK prepared to deploy RAF Typhoons to keep strait of Hormuz open after Iran war
stories.summary: Proposal at heart of offer made during a 30-country two-day meeting jointly organised by France Middle East crisis – live updates Britain is prepared to deploy a squadron of RAF Typhoons based in Qatar to patrol over the strait of Hormuz as part of a multinational mission to keep open the strategic waterway once the Iran war comes to an end. The UK military also offered to deploy mine-hunting drones and specialist divers to help clear the strait mined by Iran – but no decision has been made on w

stories.title: Baby died after NHS trust failed to warn mother of ‘unsafe’ home birth, coroner finds
stories.summary: Seven-day-old Poppy Hope Lomas died after complications during home birth encouraged by midwives at Barnet hospital A mother who lost her baby a week after an “unsafe” home birth that went against medical advice was failed by the NHS, an inquest has found. Poppy Hope Lomas was seven days old when she died at University College hospital in London on 26 October 2022 after complications during a home birth that, according to her mother, was encouraged by midwives at Barnet hospital. stories.title: D4vd possessed child sexual abuse images, LA murder prosecutors say
stories.summary: Police allegedly found images on iCloud account of singer accused of killing 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez A Los Angeles prosecutor said that the singer D4vd, who was charged this week in the killing of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, was in possession of a “significant amount of child pornography”. Police allegedly found the images on the iCloud account of the 21-year-old singer, whose legal name is David Anthony Burke. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump may talk of regime infighting, but Iran seems united by strategy born of war
stories.summary: Tensions around US negotiations may reflect mistake of assassinating more pragmatic and experienced figures Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has claimed that the infighting between moderates and hardliners in Iran’s leadership is so intense that the country has “no idea who their leader is”, but many Iranian experts questioned his analysis, saying that – given the mass assassinations of senior commanders – the country has shown remarkable institutional cohesion. In a news release , the deputy inspector general William M Blier, who the statement said is performing the duties of the inspector general, said that the

stories.title: ‘Toxic’ views of Reform UK candidates raise questions about party’s vetting
stories.summary: Hope Not Hate campaign identifies election hopefuls calling for a ‘white Britain’ and complaining of ‘kowtowing to the black community’ A Reform UK candidate who called for a “white Britain” and said Keir Starmer should be shot is among a number of contenders fuelling doubts about the party’s claim to have tightened up its vetting. The past comments of Linda McFarlane and other political hopefuls have been unearthed ahead of the 7 May elections, including one who complained about “constant kowto

stories.title: Trump administration pushes DoJ to pursue denaturalization cases – report
stories.summary: Justice department has already identified 384 foreign-born people whose US citizenship it wants to revoke The Trump administration is reportedly pushing the justice department to pursue hundreds of denaturalization cases, in which Americans born outside of the US are stripped of their citizenship. The justice department has already identified 384 foreign-born US citizens, whose citizenship it wants to revoke and will begin the process in the coming weeks, according to the New York Times . stories.title: Lebanon says Israel targeted journalist killed in airstrike
stories.summary: Amal Khalil was killed when a house she was sheltering in was hit by an Israeli airstrike. Lebanon has accused Israel of targeting media workers as part of an "established approach" — a charge Israel denies.

stories.title: Prince Harry makes surprise visit to Ukraine
stories.summary: Prince Harry has arrived in Kyiv, ahead of taking part in the Kyiv Security Forum. His trip comes as Ukrainians fear they could be forgotten with the world's attention turned to the US conflict with Iran.

stories.title: Bayern Munich on the brink of historic 'double treble'
stories.summary: With Bundesliga titles in the bag and cup finals to come, Bayern's men and women have a chance to achieve something unique in Europe. The two teams dominate in Germany and appear united in their Champions League aims.

stories.title: A booklover's dream holiday in Scotland
stories.summary: As UNESCO celebrates World Book Day on April 23, we visit a Scottish coastal town that was saved by books.

stories.title: Amnesty International paints a grim picture for human rights
stories.summary: In its 2025/2026 report, the human rights watchdog Amnesty International calls out the "predatory behavior of the powerful" and calls for action to defend the global order.

stories.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
stories.summary: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

stories.title: How Ukraine is rebuilding its energy system under fire
stories.summary: Since Chernobyl, Ukraine has lived in the shadow of nuclear disaster. Now, with Russia targeting its power grid, it's facing a new nuclear risk. The response is reshaping how the world thinks about energy in wartime.

stories.title: EU leaders discuss Iran, Ukraine at Cyprus summit
stories.summary: The two days of talks started with the approval of a massive loan for Ukraine and are also expected to address the EU budget and the fallout from the conflict in the Middle East.

stories.title: Iran: Daily life shadowed by war, scarcity and fear
stories.summary: The US naval blockade aims to put pressure on the Iranian regime, but ordinary Iranians mostly feel the effects. Many of them find their lives shaped by growing exhaustion.

stories.title: Libya oscillates between cooperation and illusion
stories.summary: For the first time, representatives from both sides of Libya's political divide are participating in Flintlock, a multinational military exercise under US leadership. Is this a tentative sign of hope for reunification?

stories.title: Germany unveils first ever military strategy for Bundeswehr
stories.summary: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

stories.title: UK: Health data volunteered by 500,000 people to Biobank charity listed for sale on Alibaba in China
stories.summary: The British government is investigating how health data volunteered to a charity for research purposes wound up for sale via three vendors on the Alibaba e-commerce site in China. One listing had 500,000 people's data.

stories.title: Samsung workers rally, call for larger share of AI profits
stories.summary: Unions at Samsung are threatening a weekslong strike if demands are not met. A production halt at one of the world's largest memory chipmakers could disrupt global chip supplies.

stories.title: Is Ethiopia bracing for war?
stories.summary: Analysts say the war in Iran has temporarily delayed an escalation in smoldering tensions between Ethiopia and Eritrea. But renewed armed conflict cannot be ruled out.

stories.title: Ceasefire extended: What's next in the Iran war?
stories.summary: With no fixed ceasefire deadline for now, the US and Iran are looking for ways to safeguard their own interests. DW takes a look at the main sticking points and explains some key issues.

stories.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving multiple injured
stories.summary: Two trains collided near Hillerød in Denmark leaving several people injured.

stories.title: US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says

stories.title: BBC visits migrant camp in northern France as new deal announced
stories.summary: A proposed three-year deal with Paris is aimed at stopping migrants crossing the Channel on small boats.

stories.title: Two runners help collapsed competitor finish Boston Marathon
stories.summary: Ajay Haridasse collapsed near the Boston Marathon finish line but was helped up by Robson De Oliveira of Brazil and Aaron Beggs of Britain, who supported him across the line as other runners continued the race. The dramatic moment unfolded during Monday’s race.

stories.title: In Tehran, money is short and a return to war looms over daily life
stories.summary: The BBC's Lyse Doucet reports from Iran, where the buzz of busy shopping streets masks deep uncertainty over the country's future.

stories.title: Pope Leo criticises Equatorial Guinea prisons as he winds up Africa tour
stories.summary: The Pope gives a frank and passionate speech, the likes of which have characterised his tour of Africa.

stories.title: Italian officials roundly reject proposal to replace Iran squad at 2026 World Cup

stories.title: Trump administration reclassifies cannabis as less dangerous

stories.summary: Beast Industries has strongly denied claims of sexual harassment and workplace gender bias.

stories.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving five critically hurt

stories.summary: The US and Iran continued seizing ships in the tense Hormuz standoff, with Tehran calling Washington's blockade a “ceasefire violation” and claiming it has been collecting transit fees for passage.

stories.title: Number of billionaires globally could reach 4,000 in next five years
stories.summary: There are now 3,110 billionaires but analysis shows ‘deep structural acceleration’ in wealth creation around world The number of billionaires in the world could reach nearly 4,000 by 2031, figures suggest, as the super-rich accumulate wealth at an accelerating rate. There are now 3,110 billionaires globally, according to analysis by the estate agent Knight Frank. This is forecast to rise by 25% over the next five years, taking the total to 3,915. Continue reading...

stories.summary: One pilot ordered to repay some of the $600,000 of damage caused by collision in 2021 South Korea’s air force has apologised for a 2021 mid-air collision involving two fighter jets, a day after auditors said pilots were taking selfies and filming during the flight and held them responsible for the accident. “We sincerely apologise to the public for the concern caused by the accident that occurred in 2021,” an air force spokesperson said in a press briefing. The spokesperson said one of the pilot

stories.title: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.

stories.title: UK, France agree 3‑year deal to curb Channel crossings
stories.summary: For the first time, the deal links some UK payments to France to performance in stopping migrants. Funding will be halted after one year if targets are not met.

stories.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several

A városi minierdők kiválóan mérséklik a klímaváltozás hatásait: csökkentik a hőmérsékletet, növelik a biodiverzitást, javítják a levegőminőséget és a közérzetünket is.

stories.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds

stories.summary: Swedish intelligence said Russia has been covering up a weak, debt-driven economy hit by war costs and sanctions, warning of rising inflation and banking risks.

stories.title: Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to Congo
stories.summary: Discussions reportedly come after Trump’s decision to stop initiative that allowed group to apply to resettle in the US The Trump administration is in discussions to potentially send up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war in Afghanistan to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a non-profit confirmed on Tuesday. The resettlement talks, first reported by the New York Times , come after Donald Trump’s decision to stop an initiative that allowed Afghans who assisted US war efforts

stories.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
stories.summary: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said. Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Ki

stories.title: British woman died in Ghana trying to recoup money from scammers, inquest told
stories.summary: Janet Fordham died in crash after travelling to see man who claimed he would help to recover money from earlier scams A British woman who was scammed out of up to £1m in a string of so-called romance frauds died in a road crash after travelling to west Africa to try to recoup some of her lost fortune, an inquest in Devon has heard. Janet Fordham was cheated of her life savings and her home over a period of five years by fraudsters apparently based in the UK, Germany, the US and Ghana, the inques

stories.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
stories.summary: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

stories.title: Iran war: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts flights
stories.summary: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

stories.title: Could Iran war trigger bigger trade crisis than COVID?
stories.summary: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts ask whether it could reshape supply chains more deeply than COVID-19, forcing companies to rethink strategies to counter further geopolitical shocks.

stories.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
stories.summary: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

stories.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
stories.summary: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

stories.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows to Germany via key pipeline
stories.summary: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

stories.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
stories.summary: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.

stories.title: Almost 8,000 people died or disappeared on migration routes in 2025, IOM says
stories.summary: The migration agency highlighted that at least around 340,000 family members are estimated to have been directly affected by the missing migrant crisis since 2014.

stories.title: UK ambassador to UAE warns of regional risk but backs UAE resilience amid fragile Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: In an interview with Euronews, British Ambassador to the United Arab Emirates Edward Hobart said the fragile US-Iran ceasefire should pave the way to a lasting peace with "a clear way forward".

stories.title: A Kashmir tourist hotspot became a bloodbath. A year on, the pain remains unbearable
stories.summary: A year after militants killed 26 people in Indian-administered Kashmir, families are trying to cope with their loss.

stories.title: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
stories.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.title: Iran seizes two cargo ships in Strait of Hormuz after three vessels fired on
stories.summary: The tanker seizures come after US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was being indefinitely extended at the request of mediator Pakistan.

stories.title: Thirteen killed in second India fireworks blast in three days
stories.summary: Earlier this week, an explosion at a firecracker factory in neighbouring state Tamil Nadu left at least 25 dead.

stories.title: Taiwan president cancels trip after African countries revoke flight permits
stories.summary: Taiwan has accused Beijing of putting pressure on African countries to close their airspace to Lai's plane.

stories.title: Central Asian leaders gather at first ever regional climate summit to discuss water crisis
stories.summary: The Regional Ecological Summit kicks off today in Astana.

stories.title: Israelis mark Memorial Day during ongoing conflict
stories.summary: Israelis marked Memorial Day with ceremonies and informal tributes, reflecting on loss while continuing daily life amid ongoing conflict and uncertainty.

stories.title: US charges anti-extremism organisation over payments to informants in hate groups
stories.summary: The Southern Poverty Law Center said it would "vigorously defend ourselves, our staff, and our work".

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV condemns ‘lust for power’ and inequality in Equatorial Guinea
stories.summary: The Holy Father of the Catholic Church made a stop in the final leg of his four-country tour of Africa. Equatorial Guinea is a secular country, with around three-fourths of its population Catholic.

stories.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards tighten grip on power as civilian leadership sidelined
stories.summary: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard has evolved from a theocratic wartime force into a dominant military-economic power. Over decades, it expanded regionally, reshaped Iran’s internal balance of power, now standing at the centre of a shifting political structure amid leadership changes and consolidation.

stories.title: Fed chair nominee Warsh rejects 'Trump sock puppet' label at Senate hearing
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh, the nominee for Chair of the US Federal Reserve, declared he has not pledged to lower interest rates to secure his position despite public calls from US President Donald Trump for immediate monetary easing.

stories.title: Los Angeles becomes first major US school district to limit classroom screen time
stories.summary: The school district, which serves about half a million students, is looking to reduce its reliance on devices after the pandemic.

stories.title: No cartels involved - but Mexico's pyramid attack prompts new concerns
stories.summary: The deadly shooting at a popular tourist site weeks before the World Cup has rocked Mexico.

stories.title: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as fuel prices surge
stories.summary: The airline is the latest to cut flights as the US-Israel war with Iran sends jet fuel prices soaring.

stories.title: Madrid hosts Experience Macao Roadshow showcasing culture, food and tourism
stories.summary: The Experience Macao Roadshow in Madrid offered visitors interactive activities, cultural performances and gastronomy highlighting the Asian territory’s tourism appeal.

stories.title: Syria's president arrives in Qatar for talks after Saudi Arabia visit
stories.summary: Syria’s president Ahmed al-Sharaa visited Doha on Wednesday for talks with Qatar’s emir on economic cooperation, investment and post-al-Assad regional reintegration.

stories.title: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.summary: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.title: Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
stories.summary: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

stories.title: Aerial footage shows widespread destruction in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: A 10-day ceasefire went into effect in Lebanon on Friday, but there have been several Israeli strikes and Hezbollah claimed its first attack on Tuesday.

stories.title: Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
stories.summary: Billionaire investor Justin Sun is suing the family's World Liberty crypto venture after spending $45m on its tokens.
